content:
the implicit 100% STR on every card makes it confusing because every other scaling starts at 0% but STR starts 100%

consider:
This DMG is increased by an additional 60% of Tentacle Damage and an additional 400% of STR

is that 5x str multiplier or 4x? Because it's 5x but people are going to read it as 4x no matter how many tooltips you put into the game for STR
there's no confusion when it comes to 50% counter scaling because counter scaling by default starts at 0%, so when a card says +50% counter scaling it's 50%

We won't need to put "This DMG is increased by 100% of STR" on anything at all because that's implied in what STR is and described in the tooltip. that's why I described something having a STR scaling as having "additional" scaling
that's why you'd have to word STR not as "an additional" but as a separate flat amount for every exception of the 100% str standard, which is why it'd have to be separate

that's why I'm saying *additional*. you don't put *additional* on any other scaling because there's nothing to imply that you're *adding* to.
STR applies 100% already, so an *additional* 200% of STR would mean STR has a 3x effect on the damage.
and that will confuse a very large amount of players and fail to work as a readibility increase
if your card says 200%, people are going to assume 2x STR
I disagree, but again I'm gonna take a break and come back on it. I appreciate the input
it doesn't matter if you personally understand it and believe it should be most logical in that format, what matters is how people read it

this is exactly why it's good that we could see we don't agree on it now rather than in a few days when we present out modifications.
Have a good rest !
and you have to understand that there are a lot of casual players and people who don't delve deeply into mechanics
agreed, gotta look out for those folks.

chasing an ideal is excellent for people who seek that ideal, but that only works in a situation where the audience is self-selecting; for example, theorycrafters will greatly appreciate damage formula breakdowns, so it's fine to have highly detailed wording and specific breakdowns located somewhere that only people who are looking for it will find it
but this is a work that gets pushed to everyone and therefore must also be comprehensible to that everyone
Do we have access to the "tutorials" text ?
yep, tutorial starts at line 57000 of the gameplay sheet
putting a more detailed numerical deep dive into that section would be fantastic, actually
though I'm unsure if the devs actually want us to do that or if they prefer it be more high-level and simplified
I think if we get a copy and separate it to work on it, we could make the divisions and later on just get the new changes to the file original file for uploading.
could you rephrase that ?

Basically I meant, to get a copy where we can have the text and work to go over with a better localization, like separate where each part/chapter starts and end, for later on we get the current one, just update de new change, and upload it on git afterwards, it is a bit more of work but I think it is easier to find out where each thing happens.
it's honestly crazy how fire some of these lines are, especially considering this is flavor text that like 99% of people will skip over
Originally I was mostly bugged by the "*the enemy*" instead of "*enemies*"
hmm the chinese is 状态, or status
but might as well touch up on the madness part to make it consistent
specifically 疯狂状态, or madness status
but as a gameplay mechanic madness is very clearly a status with stacks
...I'd just go with stacks
the existence of the word "all" necessarily implies the use of the word stacks
otherwise it'd be "remove the Madness state" without all, but all is also needed due to enemies existing who have the ability to stack multiple madness stacks
so all madness stacks is logically the only valid phrase (in my opinion)
Alright, I agree with the use of "stacks", but everyon should feel free to comment on that.
also good lord, are you working with the csv format directly in n++?
you are a madman

Currently working on a strict definition guide for this kinda stuff, **Stacks** is in there but I'll need more time~
In this case though, this is correct in the conclusion that Madness is a Status that can have one or more Stacks. <:BennettThumbsUp:773345984474120202>

Next question, the Exhaust property for cards generated is **very** inconsistent.
We have "*attach exhaust*", "*exhaust [card]*", "*[card] with exhaust*" for cards that exist or just straight up not mentionning it
yep!
I do a few things at once so it'll be easier for me to separate them into relevant commits that way
we need a agreed upon syntax for attaching tags to cards as this will come up in various mechanisms
I'm planning to retemplate the way these keywords are presented entirely, could you leave this to me and work on something else? 😅 ❤️
outside of exhaust cards can also get voided, there's the enemy melt/burn tags, there's hameln's crescendo which is a tag added to cards

"[card] with exhaust" when attaching it is in my opinion very much fine.
"[card]" with no precision is also fine for derived cards imo since you're gonna click it anyway
You're gonna need input on that anyway, why not start now ?

I have a clear picture of how I think they should be templated and it'd be easier for me to work on that all at once myself than to explain it all plus reasoning beforehand. I wanna make sure I have ample time to dedicate my mind to it and I'm a little busy on and off today so it'll have to wait for tonight.

three quick examples, one is that I'd like to change "Void" to "Fleeting", make "Fleeting" *imply* "Exhaust" in its description, and clarify that "Fleeting" cards cannot be "Retained".
two is *(I would need to collect more information on the game mechanics before locking in a decision for this, but)* I'd like to make clear terminology for the ways that the game creates Cards, using a standardized blend of "Create", "Conjure", "Copy", "Duplicate" etc.
three is I'd like to correct all instances of the language used to describe actions taken upon game mechanics, such as anything giving you stacks of a positive status should use "gain", negative status on you should use "suffer", negative status on opponent should use "inflict", etc.

and yeah, I suppose it would be best for me to just start with that work instead of the definition work I'm currently doing, thanks for the reality check. I have the kind of adhd that messes with my task prioritization skills

I just figured a comprehensive guide on how terms should be used would be helpful for people trying to work on the cards *(or double-check my work)* but I should probably focus on *actually doing said work* first

I'm not saying you should do X or Y first but rather that whatever you produce on your own will have to be approved by the volunteers (and then by Ace & BIAV) at some point, since you can't just push and entire rewrite of the system onto us out of nowhere.
So why not start explaining & debating now what you think is important, instead of spending your time making something that might be a waste since we might all disagree on

> three is I'd like to correct all instances of the language used to describe actions taken upon game mechanics, such as anything giving you stacks of a positive status should use "gain", negative status on you should use "suffer", negative status on opponent should use "inflict", etc.
Yeah, that's nice, now we can just discuss here what verb should go with what thing, and keep it in mind when proofreading stuff for example
Will do! I'll be discussing and documenting the changes I'm making along the way, no worries.

For example I'm going over Sanga's kit, shield has an inconsistent blend of "*obtain*" & "*gain*" in general, and Sanga has the 2, even mixing together in one of her skills, which is incoherent.
I'm gonna change it to only have one of the two, how about we start discussing which one ?
for the reference

Sorry to spring a question in the middle of figuring out how we're handling the mechanics, but I'm wondering how to handle the exploration events in a chapter I'm currently proofreading/editing. Should I treat the event as part of the chapter, or let someone else focus on the events exclusively?
also second number isn't colored but that's a thing i won't touch until we get some more explanations on tags :3
imo do whatever shows up in the line sections you're doing, to keep it easier for organization's sake
"obtain" is an obtuse term, and I would use "gain" in all cases of gaining Shield, as it's a combat status that has stacks.
like, if you're doing story sheet lines 14000-15000, that makes it easier to mark which sections have been covered and don't need another pass
if events are in the gameplay sheet then leave that be since we'll get to it eventually
Yeah that's what I meant, the events like say, the ones where you get whatever doohickey is the chapter's gimmick right at the start of each domain.
i would vote for the standardization of using "gain" in all instances of shield (and buff stacks in general)
I went for gain too here, especially since "obtain" is pretty much the standard for aliemus-related numbers

plus Events can have gameplay terminology ramifications, and I'll be going over all of those myself anyway.
-# *looking at you, "infect" "Symptom"...* <:angyboi:920277976883540008>
Are we going to replace all the "enjoy an X effect" instances too?
Because I don't know, it just sounds a little goofy to me.
absolutely
I'm all for standardizing the strength multipliers
I'll likely be covering those later since I'm currently doing chapter titles and stage titles and I think those lines are adjacent to the area I'm working in the sheet
some percentages, some "×X str mult", some "has X× bonus"
also I don't care about much else but I *will* fight to the death over the right to handle the ch8 starting event
as a reminder, yoou'll be only sending the things you've worked on in the end, meaning you can just not care about whatever you want
Well of course, but I want to eliminate any possibility of people doing the same work twice for example.
all lines have identifiers that (*hopefully*) will be used to merge your work, so if you don't wanna touch a thing, don't touch it and don't include it in what you commit
I basically feel the same way about Miryam's story. That despite being borderline word salad at times, was oh so compelling that I sat through over two hours of it.
miryam did nothing wrong
all hail godking tulu
Caro is my favorite realm to play, but Aequor has very interesting characters overall.
Time to get some more coffee~

I've been trying to figure out how I would template that for a while now.
We can't use gain; ("gains a 300% STR modifier") because it's not gaining us more STR.
Enjoys is kinda goofy but it gets the point across easily and quickly.
the best solutions I've come up with are quite a lot of text, so I'm hesitant to use them but
"STR has 400% effect on this DMG" doesn't easily allow for other modifiers to apply to the damage ("Tentacle DMG has a... uh, what on this DMG")
"This DMG is increased by 400% of STR" does allow for other modifiers though;
for example Tulu Strike could read "Deal N DMG. This DMG is increased by 112% of STR and 12% of Tentacle DMG."
-# while we're on Tulu I'd like to note that "Command" shouldn't be used in his Aftershock effect cus Command has a gameplay definition so I think we should change it to "Compel 1 Tentacle to attack."
> STR affects damage with X× efficiency
I'd gravitate around some variation of this (2× being +100% compared to normal)
Though it is then pretty confusing to have "This DMG is increased by 100% of STR" being implied in literally all Active DMG, and have that explicitly described in these kinds of effects.

That's what I was going for in the "STR has 400% effect on this DMG" thought, but again it'd be difficult to incorporate other DMG bonuses like Tulu's Tentacle bonus into that statement, without repeating itself entirely:
"STR affects this DMG with 1.12× efficiency, and Tentacle DMG a~~ffects this DMG wit~~" nah

the best I can come up with right now, and what I'll be basing further discussion on refining, is:
`Deal N DMG. This DMG is increased by X% of {modifier} and an additional X% of STR.`
well, tentacle damage is an entirely separate matter
just like damage scaling with shield/counter/etc
that's un unnatural "scaling" and in fact should probably be worded differently

I disagree. When an effect has additional scaling, it should be standardized so it doesn't have to repeat itself, whether it be additional scaling based on STR, Counter, Shield, Tentacle DMG, or anything else.
I'm always on the side of finding ways to condense text as long as comprehension is not lost.
The more text a player *has to* read, the worse the gameplay experience in general.

i'd say whatever unnatural base for damage would "boost" it, whereas strength always boosts damage, and only the exceptional "*this attack thinks you have 3× more strength than you do, actually*" should have its very own phrasing

> When an effect has additional scaling, it should be standardized so it doesn't have to repeat itself, 
I agree, just not for strength
oooooh boost is an excellent word for this, good thinking
Sanga's wording is also an interesting way to view it
it gets the point across very clearly, but damn if that's not a lot of words though.
whilst this is by no means compact, "*damage is increased by [mult] with [thing]*" is an interesting way to phrase it
it is but it's also unique to sanga exalt and has to be, because that's an effect with scaling that can't be updated on the card
you can't use the same wording elsewhere because it introduces confusion due to the red numbers updating based on the scaling

that's literally just what I wrote earlier though, no?
`Deal N DMG. This DMG is increased by X% of {modifier} and an additional X% of STR.`
`Deal N DMG. This DMG is increased by 300% of Shield, and has a 31% Crit Rate and Crit DMG bonus.`
if a card said "Deal 100 damage, increased by 60% of tentacle damage", that implies that 100 damage is the base and you gain an additional 60% tentacle damage to it on top
but the game code already updates the red number (100) with the 60% tentacle damage scaling to begin with, so that causes confusion
```Deal N DMG. This DMG is increased by X% of {modifier} and an additional X% of STR.``` this would work though
in combat
yeah, I just don't like the fact that you use that for str
strength being such a common mechanic with universal scaling does mean it should be treated differently

personally my suggestion would be something along the lines of:
```Deal N DMG. This DMG is increased by X% of {modifier}. X% STR scaling.```
but that reads awkwardly and sounds bad
so there's some level of tradeoff here between a card that actually sounds good to read and well, just kinda listing what it does in abrupt fragments

sure, if it was templated that way, but we're not templating it that way. big difference between
> Deal 100 DMG, increased by 60% of Tentacle DMG.
and
> Deal 100 DMG. This DMG is increased by 60% of Tentacle DMG.
example 2 implies with the full stop that the 100 DMG is the grand total, and the further explanation that describes "this DMG" is for the player to understand how we got to 100 DMG.
yeah that's what I was getting at
I was just saying that the sanga example can't be copied to other cards so we have to word it differently

> example 2 implies with the full stop that the 100 DMG is the grand total, and the further explanation that describes "this DMG" is for the player to understand how we got to 100 DMG.
Eh, that's not immediately clear to me
the "This DMG" does not bring clarity to me

I still don't see why STR needs to be treated differently, unless I don't properly understand the damage formula.
From what I understand, STR can be fully and completely described using the phrase
> Active DMG you deal is increased by 100% of your STR.
and if you want to get nitpicky,
> Active DMG you deal is increased by 1 for each stack of STR▲ and reduced by 1 for each stack of STR▼.
am I wrong?
strength would need to be treated differently because every card implicitly has 100% str scaling so adding that to every card is excessive bloat

weeeeeell
The answer is : "kind of ?"
It's in the same place for the damage formula, unless some spells have surprises and count it for base dmg scalings
that's why we have tooltips.
and there are so many cards which scale extra with STR but not anything else, so just including str in the wording for that is going to make other scaling cards sound weird
I'm gonna take a break, study this, have some food and caffeine, and come back with a fresh mind on the matter. I still don't see why `This DMG is increased by an additional X% of STR.` can't work.
tooltip changes require code changes, gotta remember that this project has no ability to impact code changes so we can only work with existing tags and text changes but nothing else

technically it could be the same, but my opinion is that strength is everywhere, and we'll never have a "100% of str" mentioned on every card, so a separate phrasing (ideally only about the difference with the norm) would be warranted
Wait we don't have access to changing the text of this tooltip?
command cards is definitely a thing, but command zone isn't
gonna be honest
didn't even know command zone was a thing
It's literally just used here I'm pretty sure.

that seems like a legacy holdover where they likely meant for it to be a thing and later just decided hey people can just play cards wherever it doesn't matter if it's a specific zone in the middle of the screen
and then never went back and cleared it from the tutorial because nobody remembered or noticed
Are command cards a thing as in, in the story?
Because I haven't encountered any instance of that so far.
well that's a gameplay tutorial line
it's very definitively a significant gameplay aspect
a command card is specifically an awakener skill card and not a state or symptom card
Alright, I'm going with my gut and just say "drag the command card towards the play field/screen/whatever"
i'd probably keep the yellow formatting and just say "center of the screen"?
thoughts?
rather than central command zone as that isn't a named thing
I was about to say this too. Center of the screen is easily understandable and won't really break immersion.
seems good to me, it certainly fits ryker's personality well
personally I don't think I'd have written it that emphatically but that's more of a difference in philosophy when it comes to editing and it's still well within normal bounds so I have no complaints

呀，让我看看这是什么好东西。"Ah, let me see what this good thing is."
(I'm tired of screenshotting)
ok what is Pandia saying here

also <@183782216353054720> one of the natural results of a volunteer project like this is that some people will have more time, some people will have more motivation, and people tend to jump on the more popular topics/aspects
don't feel like anything is "claimed" though, always feel free to share your opinion even if the discussion happened hours ago

something (in my experience) that comes up a lot is that an active discussion might consist of only 2-3 people out of a group of 20-30, and they might make a decision that they think is valid at the time, but objectively that's a very minority opinion and speed doesn't necessarily correlate to quality or correctness
opinions raised days after the fact could very well be more valid than opinions raised previously, so never be scared to speak your mind

there might naturally be some hackles raised since people tend to feel like you're stepping on their toes, as it were, if you raise a concern after they consider it a "done deal", but that's something that people need to learn to live with in a group contribution project and isn't something that should be on your shoulders
好东西 in chinese is quite literally "good thing", but it's a term that means something interesting
I'm not even sure how to approach this sentence
I checked the text for context and it looks like it's just pandia's opening text in the communicator?
based on that I'd assume she's referring to the communicator itself
as in hey let me check out what this thing is

Thanks <@88644018673582080> that means a lot <:qiqipeek:950649058324803626> 
I've never worked on a group project like this so that's probably gonna be a weak point of mine 😅
everyone has their strong points and their weak points
We're in this together! I want us all not just to get along and be productive, but to cooperate in all we do.
Firsts for everything! I'm just super glad y'all are so friendly

I might not have too much but one of my strong points is I have a fair amount of experience in group-based translation projects so if nothing else I can at least try to prevent some of the common pitfalls
So what you're saying is that we should meet up and fight to the death to decide who gets to translate any specific chapter.
https://tenor.com/view/anime-anime-girl-head-nod-head-nodding-head-nod-yes-gif-24603507
https://i.redd.it/lwfyz1z2kbjf1.gif
no items, fox only, final destination
i got quite distracted and forgot to reply, but i think Dissolution is much better
seeing as it's a phenomena and not a being
you wouldn't refer to a hurricane as "The Hurricane", though you would still use "the" in appropriate contexts ("The hurricane destroyed my house")
are we calling it dissolution or Dissolution though ?
if it's important enough it's probably capitalized
so I'd say D
Dissolution as it's a named phenomena
think Day of Reckoning
that's generally capitalized and it's a religious phenomena
well this is neat - I did not realize that the act 1 name is morimens
act1 is titled "Ere Oblivion's Dawn", which is 忘却前夜 in chinese, which is the name of the game, i.e. Morimens
I don't think there's a way to make that reference in english unfortunately, it does feel neat that in chinese the players get to see it and go
https://tenor.com/view/thats-it-yes-thats-it-that-right-there-omg-that-thats-what-i-mean-gif-17579879
Current Workloads
Back from lunch. Let's see if I can pick up the pace a bit.
don't hesitate to group up for things, exchanging your opinions on certain terms might as well be done early on
remember to crosscheck all terms used in the main terms sheet https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1QW8qr1y7DLFnTZVOHUTmw_IBL9bq8JEaIPKQGMOjzKE/edit?gid=1786279439#gid=1786279439
any suggested terms go into that column (though I'm not sure who reviews the suggested changes...)

Speaking of terms... Ramona calls the students transformed by the Dissolution "Mutants"... wouldn't something like "Dissolute" make more sense? Checking the term base, they **are** called Mutants there too, but I don't recall the game referring to them as that ever again?
mutants is used pretty often, I recall seeing it quite a bit
though a specific term for it could make sense
Huh, I must be glossing over it then.
looks like 30 occurrences of the word mutant in the story sheet
not a tremendous amount but it does get some use
I personally think mutant brings to mind something different than what these are, but if it's prevalent, might as well stick close to the original meaning.

"Those Mutants reached the Keygrave before the alarm sounded. The Mist Barrier around Mythag must be down."
The GTL for that sentence mentions the barrier being ineffective instead.
So the reason I want to clarify if they meant the barrier being down rather than it just not being effective in keeping out the Dissolution, is that they have very different implications?
If the barrier is down, that means something or someone caused it to be turned off, potentially even a traitor conspiring to destroy Mythag from within.
Does anyone that progressed further in the story know the answer to this? I feel like this is kind of an important tidbit being foreshadowed, and I want to make sure.
have you finished act1 of the story yet?
I haven't. I finished up to Chapter 5.
spoilered to be safe ||but yes, there is a traitor in mythag and one of the things he does later in the story is turn off the mist barrier||
I only read the first few words just in case. Okay, I'm keeping it as it is then~
Thanks for clearing that up!
Question : regarding gameplay terms descriptions, how pedantic should we be ?

example with **Quasar**, ~~this description is technically not true~~
edit : after verification, it seems the behavior I was thinking of has changed, and it is no longer incorrect !
my gut instinct is to say very, since the more polish the better
Yeah, I feel like in terms of gameplay, we should be as nitpicky as possible. The game is already complex enough without misleading descriptions.

Absolutely this, and I'm putting myself in charge of this kind of stuff. An understandable and approachable card game should always have a very straightforward, very strict and most importantly very CONSISTENT mechanical vocabulary for all of its effects, and not a single word should be wasted or overlooked when describing these things. <:BennettThumbsUp:773345984474120202>
Does anyone know what a "precision case" is? My searches are pointing towards cases for sniper rifles and whatnot.
what's the context?
"Doll retrieves the Silver Key from a precision case, placing it on your chest. It glows faintly, flooding you with familiarity."
MTL
The GTL calls it a "delicate locket" instead, but the short movie that plays doesn't make it look like a locket to me.
original chinese here is 精密的小盒
"a delicate little box"
You're a lifesaver. I figured it was probably some quirk of the MTL, but there are occasionally some legitimately good lines that I barely touch or skip editing altogther.
Just wanted to make sure.

personally I would try to fit in the descriptor *ornate*
-# just my two cents
honestly just "ornate case" would fit fine
Agreed.
also just to be clear real quick, I hope I'm correct in assuming MTL and GTL mean Machine TransLation or GPT TransLation?
Google Translation in this case.
Been peeking at it every line just to get a better picture of the original meaning.
Madness statuses or Madness states ?
is this referring to the enemy stacks where they hit additional times?
also known as Soulsalve in-battle or Soulsave out of battle
I'd go for stacks but that's because I'm annoying
stacks would be the correct term here from a gameplay perspective but let me check the text...
what is Lily saying here
weak ass gloves I guess
is she saying one of her pustules crawled into a balloon and is floating away?
I think she's saying "I'm at Sylvia's"?
as in her office or room
remind me who Sylvia is
one of the hot professor ladies
do you have the slighest idea how little that narrows it down
||it doesn't matter just wanted an excuse||
oh right
but yes one of her pustules crawls into a balloon and floats away and she's like oh no onee-chan help
rain goes in, drips down into glove, gets to fingertips
until I get better clarification on what exactly she means by "I'M AT SOUP"
I think this works?
though well, I know some might consider it aggressive editing but I'd honestly rewrite that entire sentence because it just does not flow well to me
yeah
unrelated side note: I know pustule is the right word here but it's a silly word and I still giggle every time I read it
they also interchanged it with boil
also I can't get over lily's phase 2 passive where she copies your first card played and if you play a non-strike/defend card she gets confused and just decides to summon a pustule
lol
it's minor but just the way that's worded is like one of my favorite things ingame
I think I cooked on Karen's, but I feel like this sentence can be written better
original
Ding-ding~ Your wonderful Maid Karen here letting you know that your breakfast's ready!
me
I feel like there's a better way to writet his
cause she's basically doing room service and probably rolled in one of those hotel trolleys with the food on it
Oh, we should check if the game's font renders a tilde by itself properly, or if we'll have to use 〜
It can occasionally be a problem.
the source sheet has it as ~
Ah perfect then.
I only added more
wait I figured it out
ok what did the keeper mean with Ryker
and the "you'll be drinking coffee"
also "not reporting me" but it's also said by the keeper so I'm assuming the keeper is saying "me not reporting you"
"drinking coffee" means to be interrogated by the police
oh
that's slang
I see
that makes sense
chatgpt is bad at catching that (for obvious reasons)
should I keep the slang
but yeah it comes from the police "inviting" you for a cup of tea
and well, you can see where that goes
I wouldn't, it doesn't translate well
it'd need to be a lot more clear in english though you can probably preserve some of the nuance

maybe something like:
Next time it ends up in Doll's hand, and you'll be having a "discussion" with the police
though the chinese text does have 你**就**等着去 rather than 你等着去
which does have somewhat of a minor tone change (to me, at least), of "just wait" rather than "wait"
so should I do like "Be more careful next time, or your message might end up in Doll's hands and you'll be in a different underground room getting grilled."
or something like that
try to preserve the implication but not outrightness
I can workshop the sentence but moreso the general idea
might be a little bit too on-the-nose
but yeah the idea is there
I also want it to connect back to his original message so I'm thinking something like "you'll be playing a different game" but that might be too ambitious
also yeah I think chatgpt just got confused here, that line says i'm reporting you, not you're reporting me
classic MTL

「没有反手举报」- not turning around and reporting (you, implied)
「我已经很讲情分了。」- I've already given you a lot of consideration

i think chatgpt got confused and parsed it as
「没有反手举报我」- not turning around and reporting me (pronoun)
「已经很讲情分了。」- (you, implied) have already given a lot of consideration
The strings being out of order are doing a number on me. Especially the combat ones that are timed, so I barely have time to alt-tab to check.
Still should be able to finish the prologue today.
I'd also like to check the events that happen in the exploration mode. Are they listed under Gameplay?
So here's another quandary.
"Hold down the <Yellow:Command Card> and drag it to the <Yellow:Command Zone> in the center to issue an action command."
Should I keep the "RP" in the tutorial message?
yep, events are under gameplay
Or just straight up say "drag it to the center of your screen?"
Because I'm of two minds here.
the ch8 starting event is at line 12162, for example
unrelated tangent but despite being chatgpt this is honestly imo one of the best lines in the game
Emdash spotted. <:point:1356053112292511815>
Joking aside, that to me just speaks of the quality of the original.
can't blame chatgpt emdash for this one
it's in the original chinese!
damn I can't come up with anything clever like the coffee one
I'd translate exactly what the text says, because it is the text that's wrong here
Maybe something along the lines of "Unless you want to become thoroughly acquainted with the inside of an interrogation room?"
Might still be too on the nose.
but I would send it to the devs to be like hey guys you kinda left this in the original text maybe you should remove it
best I managed to do was "you'll be making your future bets with toothpicks and stamps" but that's pretty esoteric
personally I think for that line you can just be more direct
chinese "innuendos" (if you want to call it that) are a lot more commonplace and normal in conversation

<@&1402540806539051120> <@88644018673582080> 

Thank you for your suggestions regarding current English terms. We appreciate you pointing these out!

**'Murloc':** This was a great catch. I'll plan to implement this change in a future update. Since it involves editing a lot of existing text, it may take some time.

**'Aliemus' for 狂气:** I was also considering 'Madness' or 'Insanity,' but our narrative designers specifically requested 'Aliemus' as the exact term. Since it has already been widely implemented, we've decided to keep it as is.

For all future suggestions, please submit any proposed changes to existing term translations in the [EN-Term Base file](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1QW8qr1y7DLFnTZVOHUTmw_IBL9bq8JEaIPKQGMOjzKE/edit?usp=sharing) by editing the 'SUGGESTED CHANGE' column. You can also add new term entries.

About this issue where Thais ult can copy Goliath ult's Derived Cards:

I talked to our combat designers; they are looking into this, might be a bug. Thank you for letting us know! This type of bug issue can be submitted in the official server's Questions channel

Someone said Tawil creates Derived cards?
Tawil exalt copies "innate command cards" (i.e., a character's base skill cards) which are in turn treated as such by thais exalt and can be copied
Tawil's own two wings/four wings/six wings are derived cards and cannot be copied by thais exalt
